attempted_route: RDKit.AllChem.GetMorganFingerprintAsBitVect(radius=2, nBits=2048) + sklearn.RandomForestClassifier via GridSearchCV(StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5), scoring='balanced_accuracy'); output written with reset_index(drop=True)
observation: exec exit_code=0, all three CSVs saved; eval exit_code=1 with ValueError: Can only compare identically-labeled Series objects on predicted_df.standardised_smiles == gold_df.standardised_smiles
rationale: subset.reset_index(drop=True) reassigned pandas index 0..N-1 in the output CSVs, while the gold DataFrame retains the original CSV row indices; the evaluator's Series equality check requires identically-labeled indices.
recommended_alternative: Remove reset_index(drop=True) from the subset copy so the original DataFrame index is preserved, and write out_df = pd.DataFrame({'standardised_smiles': subset['standardised_smiles'].values, 'label': preds}) with index=False — ensuring row order matches gold without pandas index mismatch.
